human_description_en: where search matches ""company"" sorted by published_date descending

rows.title: ACRA Alert: Beware of Scam Emails
rows.content_text:
News & announcements

ACRA Alert: Beware of Scam Emails

ACRA Alert: Beware of Scam Emails

Corporate service providers (CSPs)

Public advisories and support

ACRA wants to alert the public of scam emails, for example, with the subject headings:

"News » ACRA Notification – Intended to Dissolve Company Under Section 344A of the Companies Act [Updated]"

"News » ACRA Notification – Intended to Dissolve Company Under Section 344A of the Companies Act [Updated]"

"Regulatory Affairs Office » News » Corporate Compliance Warning Notice [Updated]"

"Regulatory Affairs Office » News » Corporate Compliance Warning Notice [Updated]"

"You have been added to Regulatory Affairs Office"

"You have been added to Regulatory Affairs Office"

These emails claim to be from ‘ACRA’. The scam emails may claim that the recipient's company will be struck off by ACRA or include a link that likely leads to a phishing website.

What You Should Know:

Members of the public are advised to ignore these phishing emails – they are not authorised nor sent by ACRA.

Members of the public are advised to ignore these phishing emails – they are not authorised nor sent by ACRA.

To report a scam, please call the Police Hotline at 1800-255-0000, or submit a report atwww.police.gov.sg/iwitness.

To report a scam, please call the Police Hotline at 1800-255-0000, or submit a report atwww.police.gov.sg/iwitness.

Members of the public are advised to stay vigilant and follow these security practices to protect themselves against scams:Always verify sender authenticityDo not click on suspicious links or download any attachments

Members of the public are advised to stay vigilant and follow these security practices to protect themselves against scams:

Always verify sender authenticity

Always verify sender authenticity

Do not click on suspicious links or download any attachments

Do not click on suspicious links or download any attachments

Stay vigilant against scam attempts!For More Help:Visitwww.scamalert.sgor call the Anti-Scam Hotline at 1800-722-6688 for scam-related advice.

Annex - Examples of Screenshots of the Fake Email
rows.summary: ACRA has issued an urgent alert regarding phishing emails impersonating the regulator. These scams utilize alarming subject lines, such as notifications of intended company dissolution under Section 344A or regulatory compliance warnings, to trick recipients into clicking malicious links. Business owners, directors, and Corporate Service Providers (CSPs) are particularly vulnerable to these deceptive tactics. To safeguard your entity, always verify sender authenticity and avoid clicking suspicious links or downloading unexpected attachments. ACRA emphasizes that these unauthorized emails are not legitimate communications. If encountered, please report such incidents to the Singapore Police Force immediately.

rows.title: Public Consultation on the Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill
rows.content_text:
News & announcements

Public Consultation on the Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill

Public Consultation on the Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill

Accounting entities

Corporate service providers (CSPs)

Accounting standards and sector regulation

Regulatory updates

This article has been migrated from an earlier version of the site and may display formatting inconsistencies.

1.	The Ministry of Finance (MOF) and the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA) propose to make amendments to the:

a.	Accountants Act 2004;b.	Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority Act 2004;c.	Companies Act 1967;d.	Limited Liability Partnerships Act 2005; ande.	Limited Partnerships Act 2008.

2.	The amendments aim to (i) prevent misuse of companies for unlawful purposes; (ii) reduce regulatory burden for companies; (iii) safeguard shareholders’ interests; and (iv) enhance regulatory regime for public accountants. Consequential and related amendments are proposed to be made to other written laws to give effect to or align with these amendments.

3.	We would like to seek feedback from members of the public on the proposed amendments in the Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill from 14 to 31 July 2025. The public can access the public consultation documents, including the draft Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill, on the REACH consultationportal. The public consultation documents are also available on the websites ofMOFandACRA.

4. We welcome feedback on the draft Bill by 31 July 2025. Interested parties can submit their comments viaFormSG.
rows.summary: MOF and ACRA are proposing amendments to the Companies, LLP, LP, ACRA, and Accountants Acts via the Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill. These changes aim to curb company misuse for unlawful purposes, reduce regulatory burdens, protect shareholders, and strengthen the oversight of public accountants. Affected entities, including business owners, accounting professionals, and corporate service providers, should prepare for potential shifts in compliance requirements. Stakeholders are encouraged to review the draft Bill and submit feedback through the REACH portal or FormSG by 31 July 2025 to influence Singapore’s evolving regulatory landscape.

rows.title: Information products available in Bizfile iShop
rows.content_text:
News & announcements

Information products available in Bizfile iShop

Information products available in Bizfile iShop

Accounting entities

Corporate service providers (CSPs)

eService announcements

This article has been migrated from an earlier version of the site and may display formatting inconsistencies.

The following information products are now available for purchase through theBizfile iShop:

1)     Register of Members (ROM)

The ROM can now be purchased at $20.00 per copy. Before accessing the ROM, please note:

If you have recently filed an allotment or transfer of shares, please wait up to 60 minutes before purchasing or accessing the ROM to ensure updates are reflected.

If you have recently filed an allotment or transfer of shares, please wait up to 60 minutes before purchasing or accessing the ROM to ensure updates are reflected.

For General lodgement (Shares) transaction, wait until after the transaction is approved.

For General lodgement (Shares) transaction, wait until after the transaction is approved.

For General lodgement (Others) transaction related to shares, updates will be available after a refiling had been made (unless otherwise advised by ACRA).

For General lodgement (Others) transaction related to shares, updates will be available after a refiling had been made (unless otherwise advised by ACRA).

2)     Business Entity Extracts

Extracts for the following transactions lodged in Bizfile from 9 December 2024 are available for purchase:

Business entity name applications

Business entity name applications

Entity registrations

Entity registrations

Annual declarations

Annual declarations

Registration of Charge

Registration of Charge

To purchase these information products, visitBizfileand access iShop via the "Buy information" tab.

Extracts for other transactions will be made available progressively.
rows.summary: ACRA has expanded its Bizfile iShop to include new downloadable information products, namely the Register of Members (ROM) and various Business Entity Extracts (such as entity registrations and annual declarations) for transactions lodged from 9 December 2024. This update directly impacts corporate service providers, accounting entities, and business owners requiring official company data. While this streamlines information retrieval, users must account for potential processing lags; specifically, a 60-minute waiting period is advised following share allotments or transfers to ensure all recent filings are accurately reflected in the purchased documents, preventing reliance on outdated records.

rows.title: Nov/Dec 2024 Issue of ACRAConnect
rows.content_text:
News & announcements

Nov/Dec 2024 Issue of ACRAConnect

Nov/Dec 2024 Issue of ACRAConnect

Accounting entities

Corporate service providers (CSPs)

Implementation Committee on Accountancy Workforce Development (ICAWD) Set Up to Promote Accountancy

An Implementation Committee on Accountancy Workforce Development (ICAWD) co-led by ACRA and Institute of Singapore Chartered Accountants (ISCA) has been set up to focus on enhancing the perception of the accountancy profession and building a sustainable talent pipeline.

This was announced by Second Minister for Finance Ms Indranee Rajah at ISCA Conference 2024 on 14 November 2024.

At the conference, Minister Indranee also announced the following initiatives:

ACRA will publish an Education and Career Guidance (ECG) Playbook for Accountancy by 2025 that will offer detailed insights on career pathways of accountants and address common misconceptions about the profession.

ACRA will publish an Education and Career Guidance (ECG) Playbook for Accountancy by 2025 that will offer detailed insights on career pathways of accountants and address common misconceptions about the profession.

ACRA is also developing a Body of Knowledge for Sustainability Reporting and Assurance to guide training providers in designing specialist programmes for sustainability reporting preparers and assurance providers, in this nascent and growing field.

ACRA is also developing a Body of Knowledge for Sustainability Reporting and Assurance to guide training providers in designing specialist programmes for sustainability reporting preparers and assurance providers, in this nascent and growing field.

ACRA published the Financial Reporting Practice Guidance No. 1 of 2024 and Audit Regulatory Report 2024 on our website in November 2024.

The Financial Reporting Practice Guidance No. 1 of 2024 addresses the impact of macroeconomic shifts and climate-related events on FY2024 financial reporting and aims to enhance the quality and reliability of financial reporting in Singapore.

The Audit Regulatory Report 2024 provides an overview of our regulatory regimes and inspection programmes formulated to drive the strategic objective of raising audit quality and upholding public confidence in the integrity of financial reporting. The report also highlights various good practices that will be helpful to the profession in strengthening their systems of quality management and addressing the complexities in the audits of financial statements.

Practitioners may wish to draw on the observations and good practices gathered by ACRA at the following links:

Read the Audit Regulatory Report 2024

Download the Financial Reporting Practice Guidance No.1 of 2024 [PDF, 415 KB]

Guidance on Performing Root Cause Analysis and Remediation Plan

ACRA issued the Audit Practice Guidance No. 1 of 2024 on 28 November 2024, providing public accountants (PAs) and accounting entities (AEs) a guide on performing an effective root cause analysis and consequently to design an appropriate remediation plan.

This guidance was issued in view of the structured monitoring programme introduced by ACRA, on PAs who receive a “Partially satisfactory” or “Not satisfactory” inspection outcome under the Practice Monitoring Programme, as well as AEs whose Quality Control Standards reviews led to either a “Partially satisfactory” or “Not satisfactory” outcome.

Audit Practice Guidance No. 1 of 2024 [PDF, 337 KB]

Upholding Corporate Governance in ACRA's Prosecution of Company Director

Upholding Corporate Governance in ACRA's Prosecution of Company Director

Annual Appreciation Event for PAOC and PMSC Members

A company director was convicted of six charges on 1 November 2024 due to breaches of the Companies Act. Guan Xu made false declarations to effect changes to the financial year end of companies and submitted unauthorised striking off applications for multiple companies. A global fine of $51,000 was imposed, and she has been disqualified from acting as a company director or participating in company management for five years.

ACRA takes a serious view of Companies Act breaches, and providing false or misleading information to ACRA is a serious offence.

Read the List of Prosecution of Cases

ACRA's e-Newsletter for professional stakeholders

This e-newsletter is intended for general information only and should not be treated as a substitute for specific professional advice for any particular situation. While we endeavour to ensure the contents are correct to the best of our knowledge and belief at the time of writing, we do not warrant their accuracy or completeness nor accept any responsibility for any loss or damage arising from any reliance on them.

Copyright © 2025 Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority. All Rights Reserved.
rows.summary: ACRA, in partnership with ISCA, has established the Implementation Committee on Accountancy Workforce Development (ICAWD) to bolster Singapore’s accountancy talent pipeline. Key updates include the upcoming Accountancy ECG Playbook (2025) and new Audit Practice Guidance No. 1 of 2024, which assists public accountants and accounting entities in performing effective root cause analysis. Furthermore, ACRA is developing a Body of Knowledge for sustainability reporting. Amidst these developments, a recent prosecution of a director for false declarations serves as a critical reminder for all company officers to strictly adhere to the Companies Act to avoid fines and disqualification.

rows.title: Director of Miyoshi Limited Fined $22,400 for Company’s Failure to Comply with Accounting Standards
rows.content_text:
News & announcements

Director of Miyoshi Limited Fined $22,400 for Company’s Failure to Comply with Accounting Standards

Director of Miyoshi Limited Fined $22,400 for Company’s Failure to Comply with Accounting Standards

Corporate service providers (CSPs)

Enforcement and penalties

This article has been migrated from an earlier version of the site and may display formatting inconsistencies.

ACRA will act against non-compliance with accounting standards to maintain confidence in the credibility of financial reporting in SingaporeSingapore, 10 January 2025– The State Court has fined Sin Kwong Wah Andrew, an executive director and the chief executive officer of Miyoshi Limited (“Miyoshi”), an SGX listed company, a total sum of $22,400 in relation to Miyoshi’s failure to recognise a $16 million impairment loss in its financial statements for financial year ending 31 August 2019.

2	Miyoshi’s audited financial statements were selected for review by ACRA under the Financial Reporting and Surveillance Programme, where ACRA reviews selected financial statements filed with ACRA to check for compliance with the accounting standards. The non-compliance was uncovered in the course of the review.

3	The $16 million impairment loss arose from a decline in the value of the company’s equity investment in a foreign company, Core Power (Fujian) New Energy Automobile Co. Ltd. Miyoshi had engaged an independent valuer to assess if the equity investment was impaired. The independent valuer’s draft report, which was later finalised by the independent valuer, with no material changes, showed that a significant impairment had occurred. Despite this, Miyoshi failed to recognise the $16 million impairment loss on the investment and overstated the value of its net assets by the same amount. This resulted in Miyoshi Group’s FY2019 Financial Statements being materially misstated, providing an inaccurate picture of Miyoshi’s financial health. Had Miyoshi recognised the $16 million impairment loss in its FY2019 Financial Statements, the Miyoshi Group’s loss before income tax would have increased by more than 30 times to $16.78 million and its total assets would have reduced by 19% to about $67.9 million.

4	Directors have a fundamental duty to provide accurate and reliable financial information. Providing investors with reliable and meaningful financial information for decision-making will bolster investors' and other stakeholders' confidence in the transparency, integrity, and quality of financial reporting in Singapore.

5	ACRA will not hesitate to take action against non-compliance with accounting standards. This is to maintain confidence in the credibility and reliability of financial reporting in Singapore. Failure to comply with section 201(5) of the Companies Act currently carries a penalty of up to $250,000. For offences committed on or before 30 June 2023, the penalty for the offence is a fine of up to $50,000.
rows.summary: The State Court has fined Sin Kwong Wah Andrew, CEO of SGX-listed Miyoshi Limited, $22,400 for failing to recognise a $16 million impairment loss in the company’s FY2019 financial statements. Discovered via ACRA’s surveillance programme, this failure resulted in materially misstated financial health and overstated net assets. This enforcement action underscores the fundamental duty of directors to ensure accurate financial reporting. For business owners and compliance professionals, this serves as a stern warning: ACRA will actively penalise accounting breaches to uphold the transparency, integrity, and credibility of Singapore’s financial reporting ecosystem.

query.sql: select rowid, id, source_url, category, title, published_date, content_text, summary, created_at from acra_news where rowid in (select rowid from acra_news_fts where acra_news_fts match escape_fts(:search)) order by published_date desc limit 6
